ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento class (FOODON_00002473); no specific fried-fish-and-beef bento subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers rice-based mixed dishes; no dedicated bento code with fried fish and beef exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for this specific bento preparation found in public browser as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical bento box serving of fried fish and beef with steamed white rice weighs approximately 350 g, providing around 592 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Fried fish and beef bento — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient record F05100058
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05100058 is a canonical HPB Singapore Foodo entry with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Core macros and micronutrients are taken directly from the HPB per_100g values. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite (fried fish + beef stir-fry over rice, SR Legacy entries 15264 and 13365) because HPB does not report saturated fat for this record; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bento combination. GI of 72 is estimated using white rice as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle (GI 72 for white rice, boiled, per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 22.01 × 3.5) / 100 = 55.5 g GL for a 350 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 132 mg per 100 g — below the 200 mg/100 g moderate-concern threshold
hypertension.guidance: At 132 mg sodium per 100 g this dish is relatively low in sodium; however, a full 350 g bento box delivers approximately 462 mg sodium, so patients with hypertension should monitor total portion size and request reduced soy sauce to keep daily sodium within the 2,000 mg target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI 72) with low dietary fibre (0.78 g/100 g) produces a rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: The white rice base carries a GI of 72 and the dish provides only 0.78 g dietary fibre per 100 g, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 55.5 per standard serving; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute brown rice or reduce the rice portion, and pair the meal with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation and red meat (beef) contribute saturated fat and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of deep-fried fish and stir-fried beef contributes an estimated 1.6 g saturated fat per 100 g and generates AGEs through high-heat cooking; patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 200–250 g, request grilled or steamed fish as a substitute, and choose leaner protein alternatives to reduce saturated fat and AGE intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef provides arachidonic acid; deep-frying generates AGEs — both amplify joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its standard form because the beef component supplies arachidonic acid that upregulates pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α, IL-6) and the deep-fried fish generates AGEs that worsen synovial inflammation; substituting beef with grilled chicken or tofu and requesting steamed fish would substantially reduce the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Fish and Beef Bento (揚げ魚と牛肉弁当) is a Japanese-style bento box meal consisting of a portion of deep-fried fish fillet, stir-fried seasoned beef slices, and steamed white rice, typically served in a compartmentalised bento container. The fish is coated in a light batter or breadcrumb crust and deep-fried until golden and crisp, while the beef is stir-fried with soy sauce, mirin, and aromatics to produce a savoury, slightly sweet flavour. The steamed white rice forms the dominant carbohydrate base. Per 100 g the dish provides 169 kcal, 7.98 g protein, 5.43 g fat, and 22.01 g carbohydrate with only 0.78 g dietary fibre, reflecting the refined-carbohydrate character of white rice. Sodium is moderate at 132 mg per 100 g. The high-GI white rice base (GI ≈ 72) and low fibre content make this dish a high glycemic load meal when consumed as a full bento box (approximately 350 g), which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The deep-fried preparation method generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and the beef component contributes saturated fat and arachidonic acid, raising moderate cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns. This dish is commonly consumed as a working lunch or takeaway meal in Singapore and Japan, and is typically halal-certified in Singapore food courts. It is sold under the HPB Singapore Foodo database code F05100058.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record F05100058 with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white rice anchor with documented analogous basis.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.6 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried fish (USDA #15264, pan-fried fish fillet) and stir-fried beef (USDA #13365, beef stir-fry) weighted by approximate component proportions (fish ~25%, beef ~20%, rice ~55%); HPB does not report saturated fat for this record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific bento combination are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish as of 2026. 3. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white rice (boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for this bento combination exists in the approved GI database. 4. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (72 × 77.0 g carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 55.5. 5. The dish is described as halal in the input dietary_tags; this is preserved as is_halal: true. 6. FoodOn FOODON_00002473 and FoodEx2 A0FJP are approximate matches; no exact ontology code for this specific bento preparation exists in public ontology browsers as of 2026.
